title: ANSERINE
description: Anserine is a histidine-containing dipeptide found naturally in chicken and other meats, closely related to carnosine. It functions as an antioxidant and may help buffer muscle pH during exercise, potentially reducing exercise-induced muscle damage and oxidative stress. Research suggests it may enhance athletic performance and support recovery when consumed through dietary sources or supplementation.

research.title: Anserine Reverses Exercise-Induced Oxidative Stress and Preserves Cellular Homeostasis in Healthy Men
research.summary: Study found that anserine supplementation effectively reduced oxidative stress markers following exercise and helped maintain cellular balance in healthy male subjects.

research.title: Optimized Curcumin, Pomegranate Extract, and Methylsulfonylmethane Reduce Acute, Systemic Inflammatory Response to a Half-Marathon Race
research.summary: Combined supplementation including compounds that work synergistically with anserine-type effects reduced inflammatory markers following endurance running events.

sentiment.summary: Users report noticeable improvements in exercise performance and recovery, with one study showing a 5% increase in cycling power. The compound appears well-tolerated with minimal side effects when consumed through natural food sources like chicken soup.

effects.label: Athletic Performance:
effects.description: Demonstrated measurable improvements in cycling power when consumed 40 minutes pre-exercise

effects.label: Dose-Response:
effects.description: Effective at approximately 46.4 mg/kg body weight of histidine dipeptides (anserine + carnosine)

effects.label: Timing Matters:
effects.description: Pre-exercise consumption (40 minutes prior) appears optimal for performance benefits

effects.label: Natural Sources:
effects.description: Chicken soup and meat-based sources provide bioavailable forms of anserine

dosage.label: Research Dose:
dosage.description: 46.4 mg per kilogram of body weight for performance enhancement

dosage.label: Food-Based:
dosage.description: One bowl of chicken soup consumed 40 minutes before exercise showed benefits

dosage.label: Natural Intake:
dosage.description: Regular consumption of chicken and meat products provides baseline dietary anserine

dosage.label: Timing:
dosage.description: Pre-exercise consumption appears more effective than post-exercise for immediate performance gains

sideEffects.label: Minimal Side Effects:
sideEffects.description: Studies and user reports indicate anserine is generally well-tolerated

sideEffects.label: Food Source Safety:
sideEffects.description: When consumed through natural sources like chicken soup, virtually no adverse effects reported

sideEffects.label: Long-term Safety:
sideEffects.description: Has a long history of safe consumption through dietary sources

sideEffects.label: No Major Concerns:
sideEffects.description: Research papers do not highlight significant safety issues at recommended doses

availability.label: Dietary Sources:
availability.description: Readily available through chicken, turkey, and other poultry products

availability.label: Supplementation:
availability.description: Can be obtained through carnosine/anserine supplements or chicken-based broths

availability.label: Natural Access:
availability.description: No prescription required; available through regular food consumption or over-the-counter supplements
